questions.explanation: 'Wanted' = past tense of 'want'. Correct: I wanted to drink water.

questions.sentence: She _____ to become a doctor.
questions.explanation: Past tense: 'She wanted to become a doctor.' All subjects use 'wanted'.

questions.sentence: Did he wanted to go there?
options: Did he wanted to go there?
Did he want to go there?
Did he wants to go there?
Does he wanted to go there?
questions.answer: Did he want to go there?
questions.explanation: In questions with 'Did', the main verb stays in base form: 'want', not 'wanted'.
'Wanted' is same for all subjects including 'we'.

questions.sentence: I did not _____ to go to that meeting.
questions.explanation: After 'did not', the main verb is always in base form: 'did not want'.

questions.sentence: She did not wanted that book.
options: She did not wanted that book.
She did not want that book.
She does not wanted that book.
She was not wanting that book.
questions.answer: She did not want that book.
questions.explanation: 'Did not' is followed by base verb 'want', never 'wanted'.
Past question: 'Did you want...?' — 'did' starts the question, 'want' in base form.

questions.sentence: He wanted _____ start his own business.
questions.explanation: 'Wanted to + base verb' — 'to' is necessary between 'wanted' and the verb.

questions.sentence: I wanted go home early.
options: I wanted to go home early.
I was wanted to go home early.
questions.answer: I wanted to go home early.
questions.explanation: 'Wanted to go' — 'to' cannot be omitted before the verb.
'Wanted + noun' — no 'to + verb' needed when the object is a noun.

questions.sentence: _____ they want to attend the conference?
questions.explanation: Past tense questions use 'Did'. Present = Do/Does, Past = Did.

questions.sentence: She wanteds to travel abroad.
options: She is wanting to travel abroad.
questions.explanation: 'Wanted' has no 's' — it is the same for all subjects.

questions.hindi: मैं बस शुक्रिया कहना चाहता था।

options.english: I want to say thank you.

options: I just wanted to say thank you.
questions.answer: I just wanted to say thank you.
questions.explanation: 'Just wanted to say' is a natural polite phrase. 'Just' adds softness.

questions.sentence: We wanted _____ information about the product.
questions.explanation: 'Wanted more information' — 'more' quantifier before uncountable noun.

questions.sentence: Did you wanted to join our team?
options: Did you wanted to join our team?
Did you want to join our team?
Were you wanting to join our team?
Do you wanted to join our team?
questions.answer: Did you want to join our team?
questions.explanation: 'Did + subject + want (base form)' — not 'wanted' in questions with 'Did'.

questions.hindi: उसने कहा कि वह मुझसे मिलना चाहती थी।

options.english: She says she wants to meet me.
options.hindi: वह कहती है कि वह मुझसे मिलना चाहती है।

options.english: She told me to meet her.

options: She said she wanted to meet me.
questions.answer: She said she wanted to meet me.
questions.explanation: Indirect speech: 'wants' changes to 'wanted' in reported speech.

questions.sentence: I always _____ to work in this field.
questions.explanation: 'Always wanted' = lifelong past desire. Common in interview contexts.

questions.sentence: They was wanted to finish the project.
options: They was wanted to finish the project.
They wanted to finish the project.
They were wanted to finish the project.
They did wanted to finish the project.
questions.answer: They wanted to finish the project.
questions.explanation: 'Wanted' is simple past. No auxiliary 'was/were' is needed with 'wanted'.

questions.hindi: मुझे किसी की मदद नहीं चाहिए थी।

options.hindi: मेरे पास कोई मदद नहीं है।

options: I did not want any help.
questions.answer: I did not want any help.
questions.explanation: 'Did not want any' = past negative. 'Any' is used in negative sentences.

questions.sentence: She _____ want to apologize to him.
questions.explanation: Past negative: 'did not want'. 'Don't' = present, 'has not' = perfect.

questions.sentence: We want to go outside yesterday.
options: We want to go outside yesterday.
We wanted to go outside yesterday.
questions.answer: We wanted to go outside yesterday.
questions.explanation: 'Yesterday' indicates past tense. Use 'wanted', not 'want'.

questions.hindi: क्या manager meeting जल्दी खत्म करना चाहते थे?

options.english: Does the manager want to end the meeting early?
options.hindi: क्या manager meeting जल्दी खत्म करना चाहते हैं?

options.english: Will the manager end the meeting early?
options.hindi: क्या manager meeting जल्दी खत्म करेंगे?

options.english: Is the manager ending the meeting early?
options.hindi: क्या manager meeting जल्दी खत्म कर रहे हैं?

options: Did the manager want to end the meeting early?
questions.answer: Did the manager want to end the meeting early?
questions.explanation: Past question: 'Did + subject + want to + base verb?'

questions.sentence: He wanted _____ call you but he was busy.
questions.explanation: 'Wanted to call' — 'to' is always needed before the verb after 'wanted'.

questions.sentence: I wanted to go but I not have money.
options: I wanted to go but I not have money.
I wanted to go but I did not have money.
I wanted to go but I not had money.
I wanted to go but I having no money.
questions.answer: I wanted to go but I did not have money.
questions.explanation: Past negative of 'have': 'did not have', not 'not have' alone.

questions.hindi: वह हमेशा विदेश जाना चाहता था।

options.english: He always wants to go abroad.
options.hindi: वह हमेशा विदेश जाना चाहता है।

options: He always wanted to go abroad.
questions.answer: He always wanted to go abroad.
questions.explanation: 'Always wanted' = consistently held past desire. Very natural expression.

questions.sentence: Did _____ want to submit the report by Monday?
questions.explanation: Subject pronoun after 'Did': 'Did she want...?' — Use subject form 'she', not object 'her'.

questions.sentence: As a child, she wanted become a teacher.
options: As a child, she wanted become a teacher.
As a child, she wanted to become a teacher.
As a child, she was wanting to become a teacher.
As a child, she want to become a teacher.
questions.answer: As a child, she wanted to become a teacher.
questions.explanation: 'Wanted to become' — 'to' is mandatory before the verb.

questions.hindi: हम एक बेहतर भविष्य चाहते थे।

options.hindi: हम एक बेहतर भविष्य चाहते हैं।

options.english: We will have a better future.

questions.explanation: 'A better future' is a noun phrase. 'Wanted + noun' — no 'to + verb' needed.

questions.sentence: They didn't want _____ to that meeting.
questions.explanation: 'Didn't want to go' — after 'want to', always use base verb form.

questions.sentence: Did she wanted to travel to Paris?
options: Did she wanted to travel to Paris?
Does she wanted to travel to Paris?
Did she want to travel to Paris?
Was she wanting to travel to Paris?
questions.answer: Did she want to travel to Paris?
questions.explanation: 'Did + subject + want (NOT wanted)' — this is the most common mistake.
